candidate_summary: Orthogonal-subspace LoRA team with dynamic per-batch gradient load redistribution across M LoRA adapters, shared low-rank base S_0 as 'safety-line' connector, scout adapter L_scout pre-fixing subspace direction, and equal-load gradient norm balance constraint.
content_words: orthogonal LoRA team dynamic load redistribution
equal-load gradient norm balance across adapters

results.name: LoRAFusion Efficient LoRA Fine-Tuning LLMs EuroSys 2026 (2510.00206)
results.description: LoRAFusion 1.96x speedup; addresses mLoRA load imbalance from variable sequence lengths.
keyword_matches: LoRA team dynamic load redistribution (mLoRA load imbalance)
equal-load balance across adapters (load imbalance fix)
results.rationale: LoRAFusion targets mLoRA load imbalance from variable sequence lengths during fusion training — overlaps strongly with team-LoRA dynamic load redistribution, but its imbalance is sequence-length scheduling not gradient-norm shedding into other adapters' subspaces. Lacks scout adapter and shared safety-line base.

results.name: Gradient-Based LoRA Rank Allocation GRPO (2605.07366)
results.description: gradient-based rank allocation under GRPO; RL gradients concentrate differently than SFT.
results.rationale: Gradient-based rank allocation per layer under RL captures the gradient-norm-driven redistribution idea, but at single-adapter rank-allocation granularity, not across a team of M orthogonal adapters with shared base and scout. Borderline functional twin for the equal-load / gradient-norm mechanism.

results.name: StelLA Subspace Learning Stiefel Manifold NeurIPS 2025
results.description: StelLA three-factor decomposition; Stiefel manifold orthonormal LoRA; geometry-aware extension.
results.rationale: StelLA's Stiefel-manifold orthonormal LoRA is the orthogonal-subspace LoRA foundation — directly covers content word 5's 'orthogonal LoRA' element. Does not include dynamic gradient redistribution, scout adapter, or equal-load enforcement, but the orthogonal-subspace team foundation is fully covered.

results.name: OA-Adapter Budget-Adaptive Orthogonal-Subspace CL (2505.22358)
results.description: dynamic budget adaptation + orthogonal subspace learning; auto-adjust per-task per-layer.
results.rationale: OA-Adapter combines orthogonal-subspace continual learning with dynamic budget adaptation — this twins content words 5 (orthogonal LoRA team dynamic load redistribution). Missing scout pre-fix and explicit shared safety-line, but core mechanism is matched.

results.name: C-LoRA Continual Low-Rank Adaptation (2502.17920)
results.description: efficient reuse of learned subspaces + orthogonality constraints minimize interference.
results.rationale: C-LoRA reuses learned subspaces with orthogonality constraints — matches orthogonal team subspaces and cross-subspace reuse/transfer aspects. Does not include scout pre-traversal or gradient-norm equal-load constraint.

results.name: CL-LoRA Rehearsal-Free Class-Incremental CVPR 2025
results.description: random orthogonal down-projection shared adapters + learnable block-wise orthogonal weights.
results.rationale: CL-LoRA's random orthogonal down-projection shared adapter is a direct twin of shared safety-line + orthogonal team mechanism — covers content words 5 and 6 simultaneously. Missing only the dynamic redistribution and scout adapter.

results.name: Shared LoRA Subspaces Continual Learning (2602.06043)
results.description: shared low-rank subspace continual fine-tuning + foundational subspace.
keyword_matches: shared base safety-line low-rank connector (shared low-rank subspace)
results.rationale: Shared LoRA Subspaces explicitly provides the shared low-rank foundational subspace connecting per-task adapters — exact twin of content word 6 (shared base safety-line low-rank connector). Candidate's own novelty claim acknowledges this is the closest prior.

results.name: Serving Heterogeneous LoRA Adapters Distributed LLM (2511.22880)
results.description: dynamically rebalance adapters across GPUs; GPU-RDMA for remote access; load redistribution.
results.rationale: Distributed LoRA dynamically rebalances adapters across GPUs at the serving/infrastructure layer — twin of the dynamic load redistribution mechanism, though at serving rather than training. Candidate's own novelty claim flags this as closest at serving level.

decision_rationale: All 8 retrieved results meet at least one hit criterion under the rule (kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7). Multiple distinct prior works cover each of the four claimed novel components: orthogonal LoRA team (StelLA, OA-Adapter, CL-LoRA, C-LoRA), shared low-rank base/safety-line (Shared LoRA Subspaces 2602.06043, CL-LoRA), dynamic gradient/load redistribution across adapters (LoRAFusion, OA-Adapter budget adaptation, Distributed LoRA, Gradient-Based Rank Allocation), and orthogonal-subspace continual learning. The candidate's own novelty claim concedes O-LoRA, Shared LoRA, Compress-then-Serve, and Distributed LoRA cover the orthogonal-subspace LoRA team paradigm. Only the scout adapter pre-fix and explicit gradient-norm equality constraint remain plausibly novel — these alone constitute a recombination, not a novel mechanism, so the candidate fails the originality bar.
